rationale: Security-hardening PR for Z3's TPTP frontend. Notably this PR REMOVES a cheat pattern that already existed in master: the prior catch block at parse_annotated `catch (z3_exception const& ex) { IF_VERBOSE(2, ... 'skipping formula' ...); ... return; }` silently swallowed semantic errors and resumed parsing. The PR replaces that with `throw parse_error(...)` which surfaces the error to the caller. The PR also turns three other silent partial-handling sites (tuple $let, tuple/list formulas, modal-tuple disambiguation) into explicit parse errors, implements actual TPTP include-clause name filtering (previously skipped with skip_balanced), and adds tests asserting all four new error behaviors. No detector fired. The PR is the opposite of cheating — it tightens unsafe code. Closure was likely for compatibility-preservation reasons (the lenient parser behavior may be preferred for ingesting noisy TPTP inputs).
